reasoning: Only about two months remain in the resolution window (October 26 to December 31, 2025). The historical base rate for Article 5 invocations is extremely low—having occurred only once in NATO's 76-year history (after 9/11). While tensions remain elevated due to the Russia-Ukraine war and recent drone incursions over NATO territory (prompting Poland and Denmark to consider Article 4 consultations in September 2025), escalating these border incidents to a formal Article 5 invocation is an extremely high threshold that members actively strive to avoid. Even in the event of an intentional or accidental strike on NATO soil, allies would highly likely utilize Article 4 for consultation first. Consequently, the probability of a formal invocation within the next 66 days is extremely low. I am forecasting at the minimum allowed bound (5%).
